name: Wild Mushroom Sauce
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces Assorted fresh mushrooms (chanterelle, cultivated shiitake, oyster)
recipeInstructions:
This sauce has an intensely woodsy flavor that would be good on pasta, roast chicken or pork.
To reconstitute dried mushrooms, place them in a bowl and cover with 3 cups of warm water. Soak for at least 2 hours or up to 8. When mushrooms are soft, drain and reserve water, squeezing out as much liquid from the mushrooms as possible. Strain the mushroom water through a double layer of cheesecloth or cloth towel to remove any sand. Reserve 1 cup of liquid for this recipe (freeze remaining mushroom water for future use). Wash fresh mushrooms and pat dry. Slice chanterelles and trim stems from shiitake; discard root tips from oyster mushrooms. Melt butter in a medium skillet over medium heat, add reconstituted dried mushrooms, garlic, shallots, and salt. Cook, stirring, for 10 minutes. Add fresh mushrooms and cook another 10 minutes. Add reserved mushroom water and sherry. Increase heat to high and cook until liquid reduces by about two-thirds. Add cream and cook until reduced to a saucelike consistency that coats a spoon.
